name: Ribadesella and Ermita de la Guía Trail
name_original: Ribadesella City Tour — circular por la Ciudad y las Playas
description: Hike the Ribadesella City Tour, a moderate 5.2-mile circular route blending urban charm, coastal views, and river scenery in 2 hours and 14
region_intro: Hiking around Ribadesella offers diverse terrain, from coastal paths along the Cantabrian Sea to mountainous trails near the Picos de Europa. The region features rugged cliffs, sandy beaches, riverine environments along the Sella River, and karst landscapes. Elevation changes vary from gentle coastal walks to more significant ascents into the hills and mountains. Best hiking trails around Ribadesella The most popular hiking route is Pico Pienzu Trail from Mirador del Fitu, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views of both the coastline and the Picos de Europa. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cliffs of Hell from Camango — circular via The North Path and the Coast, a moderate 7.8 miles (12.6 km) path. This trail provides views of the Asturian coastline and the Bay of Biscay. Local hikers also love the Caves — circular route of the MILLS, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through riverine environments and past historical mills, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Hiking around Ribadesella is defined by its coastal paths, river valleys, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Ribadesella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 450 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Ribadesella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Ribadesella?
A: Ribadesella offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 90 routes to explore. These trails cater to different preferences, ranging from easy coastal strolls to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on hikes in Ribadesella?
A: The region boasts incredibly diverse terrain. You can find coastal paths along the Cantabrian Sea with rugged cliffs and beaches, riverine environments following the Sella River, and unique karst landscapes featuring caves. Further inland, trails lead into mountainous areas, offering views towards the Picos de Europa.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Ribadesella has plenty of easy options. Approximately 36 of the routes are classified as easy. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314944141/ribadesella-and-ermita-de-la-guia-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadesella and Ermita de la Guía Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.4 km path that offers accessible views and a pleasant walk.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints to discover on the trails?
A: Hikers can experience breathtaking coastal views from areas like the Cliffs of Hell, or panoramic vistas of both the coastline and the Picos de Europa from the Mirador del Fitu. The Sella estuary provides picturesque walks, and the Ardines massif features unique karst landscapes and impressive caves. You can find more details on specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Fito&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Picos de Europa with Fresh Water&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many trails around Ribadesella are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314816303/cliffs-of-hell-from-camango-circular-via-the-north-path-and-the-coast" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of Hell from Camango — circular via The North Path and the Coast&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.6 km route offering stunning coastal scenery. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314832838/caves-circular-route-of-the-mills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves — circular route of the MILLS&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through riverine environments and past historical mills.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Ribadesella?
A: The hiking routes in Ribadesella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 450 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's diverse landscapes, which seamlessly blend coastal beauty with mountainous backdrops, and the variety of trails suitable for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Ribadesella?
A: Absolutely. Ribadesella offers several routes suitable for families. The 'Samuel Cachero's Path', for instance, is an easy circular route following the Sella River, passing old mills and bridges, making it ideal for a leisurely family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in Ribadesella are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to follow local regulations regarding pet waste.

Q: What kind of historical or cultural landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Ribadesella's trails often pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter historical limekilns on the Ardines Massif, or walk along sections of the Camino de Santiago (Coastal Way). The town itself features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/817915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Guía&lt;/a&gt; and the Sella estuary with its historical significance.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, Ribadesella provides more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314922431/pico-pienzu-trail-from-mirador-del-fitu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Pienzu Trail from Mirador del Fitu&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.6 km trail with significant elevation gain, offering rewarding panoramic views of both the coastline and the Picos de Europa. There are 7 routes classified as difficult in the region.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ribadesella?
A: Ribadesella offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and fewer crowds, making them ideal. Summer is also popular, especially for coastal walks, though it can be warmer. Winter hikes are possible, particularly along the coast, but higher mountain trails may require more preparation due to weather conditions.
tips: Santa Marina Beach is a beach in the parish of Ribadesella in the council of the same name, Asturias. It is part of the beaches of the Costa Verde Asturiana and is considered a protected landscape from an environmental point of view.
Beautiful views of the bridge that crosses the Sella River in Ribadesella
The chapel of Santa Ana is an emblem in the history of Ribadesella. It was built between the 16th and 17th centuries.
Nice walk on the way to the Ermita de Guia.
A beautiful corner of Ribadesella is this beautiful hermitage that guards the city, and that lets you see impressive panoramic views of the city from the heights.
At a 100 m vertical drop over the Ribadesella estuary, the Monte Corberu peak offers views of L'Atalaya Beach at its feet.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Ribadesella City Tour and is it suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This circular tour is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty. While many parts are easily accessible, some sections might require good fitness due to varied terrain. It's suitable for most active individuals, but beginners should be prepared for an 8.38 km (5.2 miles) walk with around 100 meters (328 feet) of elevation gain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse mix of terrain. You'll walk through cobbled streets and narrow alleys in Ribadesella's old town, transition to sandy paths along beaches like Santa Marina Beach, and enjoy promenades, including the Paseo de la Grúa, which runs alongside the Sella River.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Ribadesella City Tour?
faqs.answer: This tour combines urban charm with natural beauty. You'll see the historic center of Ribadesella, modernist mansions along Santa Marina Beach, and murals by Antonio Mingote on the Paseo de la Grúa. Key highlights include the Santa Marina Beach, the Bridge over the Sella River, and the Chapel of Santa Ana. You'll also pass the Hermitage of Our Lady of Guidance, which offers stunning coastal views.

faqs.question: Are there any good viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several excellent viewpoints. From the Hermitage of Our Lady of Guidance, you can enjoy one of the best views of the Cantabrian Sea. Another notable spot is the View of Playa de L'Atalaya from Monte Corberu, providing picturesque coastal vistas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is generally during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when the weather is mild and pleasant, and the crowds are smaller. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially for beach sections, but it can be busier.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting the Ribadesella City Tour?
faqs.answer: Ribadesella has various parking options within the town, especially near the river or the beaches. It's advisable to arrive early, particularly during peak season, to secure a convenient spot close to the start of the circular route.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact route aren't detailed, many urban and coastal paths in Spain allow dogs on a leash. Given the mix of city streets and beaches, it's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in populated areas and to respect local signage regarding beach access for pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to hike this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Ribadesella City Tour. It's a publicly accessible route through the city and natural areas.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of several other paths, including the Northern Route (Asturia), Mtb Northern Route (Asturia), and the Ruta entre playas. You'll also find it crosses parts of the Paseo de la Princesa Letizia and the Camín de Guía.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! As the route passes through the city of Ribadesella, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and bars, especially in the historic center and along the waterfront. This makes it easy to grab a snack, a meal, or a refreshing drink during or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for the Ribadesella City Tour?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend wearing comfortable walking shoes suitable for varied terrain (cobbles, sand, pavement). Bring water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a light jacket, as coastal weather can change. Don't forget your camera for the beautiful views!

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of Guidance
View of Playa de L'Atalaya from Monte Corberu
